detected_departments: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
segments.title: OCRRA Battery Recycling Kiosk Locations Debated: Youth Center Safety and Staffing Concerns
segments.summary: Continued debate from Jan 7 study session on OCRRA battery recycling kiosk locations at community centers. Council members raise concerns about placing kiosks at youth-focused centers with limited staff (2 per site). Alternatives suggested include public libraries, City Hall, and Bob Cecil Center (adult-focused). DOE grant funding tied to city placement. Items 14-15 withdrawn. Item 13 held for committee meeting.

political_signal.description: Council holds OCRRA battery kiosk agreement citing safety and staffing concerns at youth-focused community centers

speakers.name: Sisha Bur
speakers.affiliation: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
processing_notes: Fire Department detection contextual — battery fire hazards discussed, not presenting dept
Councilor Ehrenreich's first meeting — states 'I believe I have the votes' to carry item
Locations debated: Wilson Park, West Morland, Kirk Park (youth-focused, too small) vs Bob Cecil Center (adult-focused, acceptable)

segments.title: Crouse Health EAP Follow-Up: 244 Firefighters Served Over Five Years, $12,000 Budget Confirmed
segments.summary: Follow-up on Crouse Health EAP agreement from Jan 7. Fire Chief provides data: 244 firefighters utilized services over past 5 years (~49/year). Union had been paying for Crouse services after city stopped funding. Council questions whether $12,000 annual budget is sufficient; Chief confirms based on Crouse negotiations. Contract includes renewal periods for future years. Item 22 withdrawn.

political_signal.description: Council and Fire Chief both advocate strongly for dedicated firefighter EAP program with Crouse Health

processing_notes: Fire Chief referenced as 'Chief Man' in transcript — not identified by full name
244 firefighters over 5 years = ~49/year; union was paying after city stopped

segments.title: Public Safety HQ Parking Lease at 220 South Gettis and Multi-Layered Parking Strategy
segments.summary: Item 23 (Mosaic/UKG) held again. Item 25: Lease of 100 parking spaces at 220 South Gettis for public safety headquarters at $37.50/month/space. Commissioner of Assessment presents multi-layered parking strategy: short-term Gettis lease (asphalt ready), mid-term train track lot (needs clearing, grading, paving — not feasible until spring). Council questions lot capacity and notes train track site appears limited. Per-space pricing ensures city pays only for usable spaces.
processing_notes: Parks and Fire dept detections are contextual — discussion references fire dept items, not presenting departments
Matt OJ likely transcription artifact for Commissioner of Assessment name
Item 23 (Mosaic/UKG) held — later goes to Finance Committee Feb 3
Train track lot = longer-term parking solution requiring spring construction

agenda_descriptions:
Local Law ~ Amend — “Local Law #5 (03/17/2011), of the City of Syracuse to adopt
the NYS Uniform Fire Prevention and Building Code which includes the latest
editions of the following codes: Building, Residential, Existing Building, Fire,
Plumbing, Mechanical, Fuel/Gas, Energy Conservation Constructi...

Sell — All right, title and interest of the City of Syracuse in and to the premises known
as 1521 South Salina Street, a masonry building, to the Greater Syracuse Property
Development Corporation for the total sum of $326 (District 4).

Special Permit — To establish a “Utility Major’ to establish Battery Energy Storage -
System (BESS) on the property located at 426 East Brighton Avenue. No one spoke
in favor, and no one spoke in opposition to the proposal. Owner, Joseph Tesiero.
Applicant, Terrence Nolan.

Special Permit — To establish a funeral home on the property situated at 4400 South
Salina Street & West Glen Avenue. One (1) person spoke in favor, and one (1)
person spoke in opposition to the proposal. David P. Colegrove, LaBella Associates,
applicant. 4400 South Salina Street LLC, owner.

Advertise Public Hearing — Relative to authorizing a six (6) month moratorium on any
zoning or permit approvals of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) within the
City of Syracuse (Public Hearing will be held Monday, January 26, 2026 at 5:30 PM)

detected_departments: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
segments.title: OCRRA Battery Recycling Safety Deep Dive: DOE Grant Deadline, Fire Prevention Partnership, and Staffing Debate
segments.summary: Extended discussion on OCRRA battery kiosk safety. Kiosks use fire-protective materials (cell block/pour-over) with 20-year zero-incident track record at Wegmans. Fire Chief Monz confirms fire prevention team collaborated for approximately one year. Key risk: batteries in trash compactors, not in kiosks. Council concerns center on adding unpaid responsibilities to underpaid Parks staff at small youth centers. DOE grant has Q1 2026 go/no-go deadline — losing kiosk placement means losing all education funding and fire department training kits. Councilor Ehrenreich cites child hospitalized from discarded button battery. Eventually held for committee meeting.

political_signal.description: Council holds OCRRA kiosk agreement despite DOE grant deadline pressure, citing youth safety and Parks staffing equity concerns

speakers.name: Sisha Bur

DPW referenced for solid waste crew safety — batteries in trash compactors
Bob Cecil Center identified as acceptable adult-focused location
